title: Supply-chain axis: scanner kinds + nonzero-exit fix (G9) + offline diff inspector (G5)
description: Add the supply-chain/dependency/secret axis. Implement the G9 fix (scanners whose convention is nonzero-exit-means-findings are parsed instead of discarded) via a per-kind scannerExitPolicy, add npm-audit/osv-scanner/gitleaks parser kinds, and build the always-available OFFLINE SupplyChainDiffInspector that flags lifecycle-script/lockfile/.npmrc/CI-workflow risks over the diff with zero network. Network-capable scanners run only behind config.security.egress.onlineResearch (default off). Findings fold into the finder's priors.

successCriteria.description: scannerExitPolicy(kind) returns 'nonzero-means-findings' for npm-audit/osv-scanner/gitleaks (and semgrep-with-error convention) and 'zero-clean' otherwise; runOneScanner (security-scanners.ts:355) parses stdout regardless of exit code for 'nonzero-means-findings' kinds, while ENOENT/thrown/abort still yield [] per-scanner (isolation preserved). A test injects a runner exiting nonzero WITH findings on stdout and asserts the findings survive (G9 fixed), and a runner ENOENT yields [].

parseNpmAuditOutput, parseOsvOutput, parseGitleaksOutput are pure, total (malformed => []), and map into SecurityFinding[] with vulnClass 'supply-chain' (or 'secret-handling' for gitleaks) and evidence path+line where available; detectScannerKind recognizes the three new kinds by type/label/command substring. Each parser is table-tested against a representative real-shaped JSON payload.

config.security.supplyChain is an OPTIONAL object {enabled: boolean (default false), scanners: EvalStrategy[] (default [])} and config.security.egress is {onlineResearch: boolean (default false)}; a config omitting them parses byte-identical; network-capable scanners (npm-audit/osv-scanner) are only invoked when egress.onlineResearch === true.

SupplyChainDiffInspector.inspect({projectRoot, diff, signal}) performs OFFLINE, zero-network checks scoped to the diff's changed files and emits SecurityFinding[] (vulnClass 'supply-chain') for at least: added/changed package.json lifecycle scripts (preinstall/install/postinstall/prepare) with obfuscated/eval/base64 content, lockfile 'resolved' host != the registry host, .npmrc registry override or ignore-scripts disabled, a new dependency with no matching import in the diff, CI workflow using 'npm install' instead of 'npm ci', and a GitHub Action pinned by tag instead of full SHA; never throws.

When config.security.supplyChain.enabled, runSecurityAudit folds runScannerPreFilter output AND SupplyChainDiffInspector output into effectivePriors alongside existing priors; the offline inspector runs even when no external scanners are configured; a test shows a diff adding a malicious postinstall produces a supply-chain prior reaching the finder.
npm run build + typecheck + lint pass; full suite green; default behavior (supplyChain absent) unchanged and no network calls occur in any test (offline inspector + injected runners only).

nonGoals: Do not add the LLM verifier (sprint 8).
Do not turn on egress.onlineResearch anywhere (network scanners stay opt-in and off).
Do not implement a full SBOM/provenance verifier (offline diff inspector heuristics only this build).
Do not add a dedicated supply-chain LLM sub-auditor (ADR-4: deterministic scanners + offline inspector, findings feed the finder as priors).
stopConditions: G9 nonzero-exit fix proven by an injected-runner test.
Offline inspector flags the postinstall/lockfile/.npmrc/CI cases with zero network; default path byte-identical.
definitionOfDone: The supply-chain axis exists: per-kind exit policy fixes G9, three new scanner parsers, and an always-available offline diff inspector fold supply-chain findings into the finder priors, all default-off and network-gated, with the suite green.
assumptions: runOneScanner + detectScannerKind + parseScannerStdout live at security-scanners.ts:260-369; the nonzero-exit discard is the branch at security-scanners.ts:355.
EvalStrategySchema (used by the existing scanners field) is reused for supplyChain.scanners; the existing scanners field and the new supplyChain.scanners may both feed runScannerPreFilter.
The repo's existing egress-axis pattern (research/medical online-research axis) is the precedent for egress.onlineResearch default-false gating.
generatorNotes: (1) security-scanners.ts: add scannerExitPolicy(kind); in runOneScanner, branch on it at line ~355 — for 'nonzero-means-findings' kinds, parse stdout even when exitCode!=0 (but still [] on ENOENT/thrown/abort — distinguish 'process ran and exited nonzero with output' from 'failed to spawn'). Add npm-audit/osv-scanner/gitleaks to detectScannerKind and parseScannerStdout. (2) parseNpmAuditOutput: npm audit --json shape {vulnerabilities:{...}} or advisories; parseOsvOutput: osv-scanner --format json {results:[{packages:[{vulnerabilities}]}]}; parseGitleaksOutput: gitleaks --report-format json array [{File,StartLine,RuleID,Secret...}]. All defensive/total like parseSlitherOutput. (3) schema.ts: add security.supplyChain + security.egress optional objects. (4) supply-chain-inspector.ts: pure-ish offline checks over diff.changedFiles — inspect package.json hunks for scripts.{pre,post}install/prepare with base64/hex/eval; lockfile resolved-host mismatch; .npmrc; CI yml npm install vs ci + action tag-vs-sha. Read files via node:fs/promises scoped to changed paths; never throw. (5) security-auditor-agent.ts: when supplyChain.enabled, fold both scanner priors and inspector findings into effectivePriors (extend the existing effectivePriors folding at security-auditor-agent.ts:86-104). Network scanners only when egress.onlineResearch. Source detection heuristics from .bober/research/research-20260714-...-research.md section D checklist.
evaluatorNotes: sc-7-1: inject a runner returning {exitCode:1, stdout:<valid findings json>} for kind npm-audit and assert findings parsed (G9); inject ENOENT and assert []. sc-7-2: table-test each parser on a real-shaped payload and on garbage (=> []). sc-7-4: craft an AuditDiff whose changedFiles include a package.json hunk adding a postinstall with a base64 blob, a lockfile with a non-registry resolved host, an .npmrc with ignore-scripts=false-removed, a CI yml with 'npm install' and an action@v4 tag — assert a supply-chain finding for each; confirm zero network (no real npm/osv calls). sc-7-3: deep-equal config-omitting parse; assert network scanner skipped when egress off. sc-7-6: build+typecheck+lint+full suite; confirm no network in tests.
